channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Three Toronto police officers arrested in Barcelona while on vacation
item.description: Three Toronto Police Service officers have been arrested by authorities in Barcelona, Spain, while they were vacationing in the city. The Toronto Police Service confirmed the arrests in a statement on...

item.title: Married at First Sight UK contestants allege sexual assault and rape during filming
item.description: Several contestants from the UK version of the reality television show Married at First Sight have come forward with serious allegations of rape and sexual assault during the production of the series....

item.title: ADSC Study Shows Historical Roots of South African Crime Precede Nigerian Migration
item.description: A recent study by the Africa Development Studies Centre (ADSC), led by Sir Victor Walsh Oluwafemi, challenges long-held perceptions that Nigerians introduced organised crime to South Africa. The resea...

item.title: OSHA Opens Investigation into Worker Death at SpaceX Starbase Launch Site in Texas
item.description: A worker died at SpaceX’s Starbase facility in South Texas early on May 15, 2026, prompting the Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) to launch a formal investigation. The incident occu...

item.title: Trump Administration Pauses Canada-U.S. Joint Defence Board Over Spending Disputes
item.description: The U.S. Trump administration has announced a pause in its involvement with the Permanent Joint Board of Defence, a body established in 1940 under the Ogdensburg Agreement between Canada and the Unite...

item.title: Mobile-first operators are transforming South Africa’s online sports betting landscape
item.description: Over the past five years, South Africa’s consumer internet has shifted firmly towards mobile, with over 70% of adults owning smartphones and mobile data becoming more affordable. This transformation h...

item.title: Quezon City man caught stealing diesel from private compound
item.description: In Barangay Payatas, Quezon City, a 28-year-old man named Bernard was arrested on Sunday night for allegedly stealing diesel valued at P2,000. Police apprehended Bernard after he was seen exiting a ga...

item.title: Gauteng Department of Health Accelerates Recruitment and Repairs at Tembisa Hospital
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Health is fast-tracking the recruitment of medical, nursing, clinical support, therapeutic services, and administrative staff at Tembisa Hospital to ensure uninterrupted heal...

item.title: Governor Uzodimma Wins APC Imo West Senatorial Primary, Defeats Ex-Governor Okorocha
item.description: For Imo State, na Governor Hope Uzodimma don officially become APC candidate for Imo West Senatorial District after he collect total of 230,464 votes. Na retired Rear Adm. Williams Kayoda, wey be Chai...

item.title: Former Qantas executive plans new low-cost airline to boost domestic competition
item.description: Peter Kelly, a former Qantas executive, is backing a new ultra-low-cost Australian airline called Zinc, aiming to offer the cheapest domestic flights between Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane, with plan...

item.title: Everyday Australians Embrace Practical Preparedness and Self-Sufficiency
item.description: Across Australia, a quiet shift is happening in how people approach preparedness. Once labelled as 'preppers', those planning for emergencies or disruptions are now seen as sensible, not eccentric. On...

item.title: The Benefits and Risks of Brain-Computer Interfaces
item.description: Brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) are emerging technologies that translate electrical signals from the brain into commands a computer can understand, allowing users to move devices, communicate, and pe...

item.title: Calgary police investigate targeted death at Sunridge hotel
item.description: Calgary police have launched a homicide investigation following the discovery of a man's body at the Emerald Hotel and Suites in the Sunridge community. Officers responded around 11:10 p.m. on Sunday ...

item.title: U.S. Pauses Joint Defence Board with Canada Following Carney's Davos Speech
item.description: The United States government has suspended the Permanent Joint Board on Defense with Canada, a move seen as a response to Prime Minister Mark Carney’s recent speech at the World Economic Forum, which ...

item.title: LTO suspends 2 enforcers over gasoline transfer in impounding area
item.description: The Land Transportation Office (LTO) has suspended two of its enforcers after a photo showing them transferring gasoline to a vehicle within the LTO impounding area went viral on social media. The age...

item.title: WeBuyCars Reports Growth Through Increased Vehicle Sales and New Locations
item.description: WeBuyCars has shown strong growth in the first half of 2026, buying 95,328 vehicles and selling 93,519, as South Africans continue favouring pre-owned cars. March saw a record monthly sale of 17,209 u...

item.title: Twisha Sharma's Death: Family and In-laws Present Conflicting Claims Over Circumstances
item.description: Twisha Sharma, a 33-year-old woman from Noida, was found dead at her marital home in Bhopal’s Katara Hills area on May 12, 2026, just five months after marrying lawyer Samarth Singh. Her parents have ...

item.title: Israel detains six Canadians near Cyprus during flotilla operation
item.description: Israeli authorities have detained six Canadian citizens near the coast of Cyprus during a maritime operation involving a flotilla, according to the organizer of the mission. Details regarding the Cana...

item.title: Launch Design and Hybrid Motors to build electric vehicle factories in Lagos and Abuja
item.description: Launch Design Shanghai don join body with Hybrid Motors Nigeria to start electric vehicle (EV) production for Nigeria. Dem don sign strategic agreement for Shanghai wey go make dem build EV manufactur...

item.title: Calcutta High Court allows TMC candidate Jahangir Khan to contest Falta repolling without coercive action
item.description: The Calcutta High Court has directed the West Bengal Police not to take any coercive action against Trinamool Congress leader and Falta Assembly constituency candidate Jahangir Khan till the repolling...
